recipes.title: Girl Scout Samoa Cookies
instructions: Start by preheating your oven to 350 degrees and lining a couple of baking sheets with parchment paper. In the bowl of your stand mixer, cream together the softened butter and sugar until the mixture is smooth and fluffy.

Add the vanilla and milk to the butter mixture and mix until everything is well combined. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt, then gradually add this to the wet ingredients, scraping down the sides often to ensure everything is incorporated.

Transfer your cookie dough to a lightly floured work surface and roll it out to about ¼ inch thickness. Cut out 2 ½ inch circles and place them on your prepared baking sheet, then use a 1 inch round cutter to remove the center of each cookie to create that classic ring shape. Pop the cookies in the freezer for about 10 minutes to firm up before baking.

Bake the cookies for approximately 10 minutes or until they're just set but not overly brown. While the cookies bake, toast your coconut in the oven for about 10 minutes, stirring several times to prevent burning.
In a large frying pan, combine the unwrapped caramels and evaporated milk over low heat, stirring frequently for about 10 minutes until smooth and melted.
Spread a thin layer of this caramel over each cooled cookie with a spatula.

Mix the toasted coconut into the remaining caramel until well coated, then working quickly with gloved hands, place about a tablespoon of the coconut mixture on top of each cookie and shape it to fit.
Melt the chocolate with the vegetable oil until smooth, dip the bottoms of the cookies in the chocolate, then drizzle the remaining melted chocolate over the tops in a zigzag pattern.
Leave the cookies to dry or enjoy them while the chocolate is still soft.
recipes.prepNotes:
Chill the dough – Freezing the cut cookies for 10 minutes before baking helps them hold their shape and prevents spreading.
Watch the coconut – Toasted coconut can go from golden to burnt in seconds, so stir it frequently and watch it closely.
Work quickly with caramel – The coconut-caramel mixture hardens as it cools, so shape it onto the cookies while it’s still pliable.
Use parchment paper – This makes cleanup a breeze and prevents the chocolate-dipped bottoms from sticking.

recipes.title: Girl Scout Thin Mints Cookies
instructions: *Note: you will need to chill the rolled out cookie dough for at least 3 hours in the fridge or 1 hour in the freezer. Make sure to clear a space in the fridge or freezer so once it's time to get chillin, the open space will be ready!
Whisk the flour, cocoa, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt in a mixing bowl. Set aside.
Beat together the butter and sugars using a hand mixer or stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment for 2-3 minutes until smooth.
Add the egg, egg yolk, vanilla, and peppermint extract.
Mix again until smooth and combined, scraping down sides of bowl as needed.  Gradually add in the dry ingredients.
Mix on a medium-low speed until incorporated and dough comes together.

Divide the dough in half. Working with one half at a time, place half of dough down on a piece of parchment paper. Place another piece of parchment paper over the top. Roll into 1/4" thickness. Repeat this with the other half of dough. Dough might be just a little sticky but it's easy to work with.

Leave the dough between the parchment paper and place on a baking sheet. You can stack both pieces of rolled out dough since they're protected by the parchment. Refrigerate for at least 3 hours or freezer for 1 hour. You can chill for up to 48 hours prior to baking, just be sure it's covered.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats.
Remove the top layer of parchment paper. Cut out the cookies using a 2-inch circular or scalloped cookie cutter.

Place cookies on prepared baking sheet about 1 1/2 inches apart. Gather and re roll the scraps, chill dough if you feel it has gotten warm, and continue to cut out the cookies and bake with remaining dough.

Bake for 8-10 minutes. Cool on the baking sheet for 5-10 minutes then transfer to wire rack to cool completely. (Note: These cookies may have slightly soft texture at first, but get that nice crisp crunch as they cool and even better if you freeze them!)
Add the melting wafers and oil to a microwave safe bowl.
Melt in 30 second intervals, stirring in between, until melted and smooth. Then add the peppermint extract and stir to combine.
Using a fork, dunk cooled cookies into the chocolate being sure to coat the cookie completely. Gently tap off and excess chocolate and place the cookies back on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Allow the chocolate to set. To store, I like to place them in the freezer because I think they're best cold!
recipes.prepNotes: Note: use Peppermint Extract  (not mint extract) (use less or more depending on how peppermint-y you want your cookies)

recipes.title: Girl Scout DoSiDo Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 180C. Line a baking sheet with parchment. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat butter, brown sugar, peanut butter and butter until creamy. Beat in the eggs and vanilla.
Mix flour, baking soda, baking powder, coconut and oats in another bowl; mix well. Add this to the peanut butter mixture, beating at low speed until well blended. Chill for 30 minutes.

Drop by level tablespoon onto prepared sheets spacing 2-inches/5 cm apart. Bake 10-12 minutes or until edges are beginning to get golden brown. Cool on sheets 2 minutes then remove to wire rack to cool completely.
Beat all filling ingredients together until the mixture is creamy. You may have to add a drop or 2 more milk to get the proper consistency--it should be like a soft frosting.
Place about 1-2 tablespoons of filling on half the cookies (on the side that's flat that was the down side on the sheet) then top with another cookie and press so the filling shows around the edges.
Yield varies but I got 32 sandwich cookies.

recipes.title: Girl Scout Tagalong Cookies
instructions: Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium-sized mixing bowl, combine flour and salt.
In a separate mixing bowl, cream the butter and sugar using an electric hand or stand mixer. Mix until smooth and fully combined.
Add egg and vanilla. Mix again until smooth.
Add flour mixture in, half at a time, and mix until fully combined and dough-like.
Remove dough from the bowl and place it on the baking sheet with parchment paper. Using your hands, flatten the dough onto the baking sheet pressing downward.
Use a rolling pin to smooth and roll out the dough to about ¼ - ½ inch thick.
Place the rolled out dough in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
When the chill time is complete, remove the dough from the refrigerator and preheat the oven to 350°F. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.
Using a 2-inch cookie cutter, cut rounds from the dough and place them onto the fresh sheet of parchment paper, 2 inches or more apart.
Bake for 9 to 11 minutes or until the edge of the cookie becomes golden brown. The cookies will spread a bit during cooking.
Allow the cookies to cool on the pan completely until they have reached room temperature.
Once the cookies are at room temperature, prepare your peanut butter mixture.
In a medium-sized mixing bowl, combine peanut butter, powdered sugar and butter using an electric hand or stand mixer. Mix until smooth and thick.

Use a butter knife or your fingers to spread the peanut butter over the top of the cookie. It should mildly adhere to the cookie however you will want to press the mixture into the cookie, ensure it is fully covered. Do this for each cookie, one by one, place them back onto the baking sheet.
When all cookies have a peanut butter layer on top, place the baking sheets into the refrigerator to chill for about 30 minutes.
Near the end of the chill time, prepare the melting chocolate.

Add the chocolate chips to a microwave safe bowl. Microwave on 50% power for 1 minute. When done, stir well and add the vegetable oil. Microwave on 50% power for 30 second intervals, stirring in between, until the chocolate is silky smooth.
Use a fork and spoon to coat the cookie in chocolate. Hold the cookie on the fork and use the spoon to scoop the melted chocolate, pouring it over the cookie, coating it completely.
Do this one by one, placing each cookie back on the sheet of parchment paper after it’s covered.
When all cookies are covered in chocolate, place them back in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Store these cookies in the refrigerator when not serving. These cookies are best served chilled.

* Peanut Butter Texture: When you’re attempting to spread your peanut butter mixture onto the cookie, if the peanut butter does not adhere well or cracks, add a bit more butter (1 tablespoon at a time) to the overall mixture. However, it should spread well despite its thickness. Press the peanut butter mixture down onto the cookie with your fingers.

* Presentation Tip: After the cookies come out of the oven, they may spread a bit. Use a large cookie or biscuit cutter to ring the edges of the cookies, smoothing them out and tightening them up a bit. For perfect edges, you can also use a cookie cutter to cut the round of the cookie, throwing away the excess outer ring that may be grown while baking.

* Cookie Texture: Once the cookies are at room temperature, they should be able to stand up on their own, without folding over. They will be a soft cookie but should still have a slight crunch and maintain their shape.

*Chocolate Coating: Use the back of the spoon to push the chocolate over the cookie and around it. You want to pour a good bit over the cookie but then carefully shake the cookie side to side to evenly disperse the chocolate. Do not dip or dunk these cookies. The chocolate acts as the glue, holding the cookie and peanut butter together.

recipes.title: Girl Scout Lemonades Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a medium-sized mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder and salt with a fork. Mix well and set aside.
In a separate mixing bowl, cream together the butter and sugar using an electric hand or stand mixer. Mix until smooth and fully combined.
Add egg and lemon extract. Mix again until smooth.
Add flour mixture in, half at a time, and mix until fully combined and dough-like.
Place cookie dough in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes before you roll out the cookie dough.
Remove dough from the bowl and place it on a flat, lightly floured surface.
Using your hands, flatten the dough pressing downward.
Use a rolling pin to roll out the dough to about 1/4 to 3/8 inch thick.
Using a 2-inch round cookie cutter, cut rounds from dough and place the cutouts on the prepared baking sheets at least 2 inches apart.

Using a lemon cookie stamp or an inspirational phrase cookie stamp, create your pattern on the top of the cutouts. Press the stamp firmly into the dough until the indentations make it at least half way through.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes.
When done, allow the cookies to cool completely on the pan before making the icing.

In a small mixing bowl, combine the powdered sugar, lemon extract, water and about 2 inches of yellow gel food coloring. Mix with a fork until fully combined and smooth. It will be very thick at first.

When the icing is at the perfect consistency, spoon out about 1 tablespoon onto the back (non-pressed side) of the cookie. Use your spoon to push the icing around so it naturally levels and covers the entire surface.
Do this for each cookie, working one at a time.
When all of your cookies are covered in icing, allow them to sit at room temperature for 6 hours or overnight so that the icing completely hardens before serving.
recipes.prepNotes:
Icing Tip: The icing can react differently depending on where you live and what type of powdered sugar you use. To create icing that hardens, stays in the cookie, is mess-free, and tasty; you need to nail the consistency. This is done by adding more powdered sugar or water/extract as needed. The consistency needs to be thick, slow to fall off a spoon, and the coloring should be more matte than glossy.
Perfect Icing: When the icing is perfect, you will be able to spoon out 1 tablespoon and not have it be runny or pour over the cookie. It should spread well but needs a little encouragement from your spoon. If your icing isn’t like this while spooning out onto the cookies, adjust your ingredients.
Cookie Texture: Once the cookies are at room temperature, they should be able to stand up on their own, without folding over. They will be a soft cookie but should still have a slight crunch and maintain their shape.

recipes.title: Ooey Gooey Peanut Butter

ingredients.name: Splenda sugar free  multi use syrup

instructions: Whisk all dry ingredients together first.
Add maple syrup, Splenda syrup/vegetable glycerin, vanilla extract.
Add almond milk gradually while whisking until it thickens to caramel consistency.
Let sit 5-10 minutes to thicken.
Chill 15-30 before use.

recipes.title: Ooey Gooey Caramel Swirls
instructions: Whisk all dry ingredients together first.
Then add wet into bowl and stir well.
Let it sit 5-10 minutes to thicken.
Chill 15-30 before use.

recipes.title: Ooey Gooey Chocolate

ingredients.name: Splenda sugar free multi use syrup

instructions: Whisk all dry ingredients first.
Add maple, syrup, glycerine and vanilla.
Add almond milk gradually while whisking until it thickens to caramel consistency
Let sit 5-10 minutes to thicken.
Chill 15-30 minutes before use.

recipes.title: Ooey Gooey Cinnamon Swirls
instructions: Whisk all dry ingredients together first.
Then add wet into bowl and stir well.
Let sit 5 minutes to thicken.
Chill 15-30 before use.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Pecan Pralines
instructions: Prepare a baking sheet by lining it with parchment paper and set aside.
In a medium-size heavy pot, combine the sweeteners, salt, half and half, and butter. Cook and stir with a wooden spoon over medium heat until the sugars dissolve and the mixture comes to a boil.
Continue to cook until the temperature reaches between 235-240°F on a candy thermometer (soft ball stage). Remove from heat and allow the praline mixture to cool for 5 minutes.

Stir in the vanilla and pecans, beating by hand with a wooden spoon just until the candy begins to lose its glossiness and thickens slightly, about 2 minutes. If you beat too long, the candy will seize and start to crumble. If you don’t beat it long enough, then pralines won’t set properly and will stay soft and sticky.

Work quickly to drop heaping tablespoons (I use a small cookie dough scoop) of pecan praline mixture onto the parchment paper-lined baking sheet. If the praline mixture begins to harden in the pan, add a teaspoon of hot water at a time and stir to keep the mixture loose enough to scoop and drop.
Cool completely until set and the pralines have reached room temperature. Store in an airtight container on the counter for 2 weeks or freeze for up to 2 months.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Pecan Pralines
instructions: Prepare a baking sheet by lining it with parchment paper and set aside.
In a medium-size heavy pot, combine the sweeteners, salt, half and half, and butter. Cook and stir with a wooden spoon over medium heat until the sugars dissolve and the mixture comes to a boil.
Continue to cook until the temperature reaches between 235-240°F on a candy thermometer (soft ball stage). Remove from heat and allow the praline mixture to cool for 5 minutes.

Stir in the vanilla and pecans, beating by hand with a wooden spoon just until the candy begins to lose its glossiness and thickens slightly, about 2 minutes. If you beat too long, the candy will seize and start to crumble. If you don’t beat it long enough, then pralines won’t set properly and will stay soft and sticky.

Work quickly to drop heaping tablespoons (I use a small cookie dough scoop) of pecan praline mixture onto the parchment paper-lined baking sheet. If the praline mixture begins to harden in the pan, add a teaspoon of hot water at a time and stir to keep the mixture loose enough to scoop and drop.
Cool completely until set and the pralines have reached room temperature. Store in an airtight container on the counter for 2 weeks or freeze for up to 2 months.

recipes.title: Soft chocolate chunks (Mixin)
instructions: Melt both, stirring together. Dig a hole off center and pour into ice cream after first spin.
Refreeze 5-10 minutes
Respin on Mixin setting

recipes.title: Keto Peanut Butter Balls
instructions: Add the peanut butter, butter, sweetener, and vanilla to a mixing bowl and beat on medium speed until well combined.
Stir in the chocolate chip by hand.
Use a small cookie scoop to scoop out 24 balls of dough. Roll the dough between your hands to shape into a ball.
Place on a baking sheet in the refrigerator to firm up for one hour.
Store tightly covered in the refrigerator.

recipes.title: Chicken Cutlets
instructions: Cut the chicken breast in half, lengthwise. Use the smooth edge of a meat tenderizer to flatten the chicken until the thickness of the chicken is even.
Whisk the eggs in a shallow bowl and set aside.
In a separate shallow bowl, whisk together the panko, grated Parmesan, salt, Italian seasoning, garlic powder and pepper.
Dip each chicken breast into the egg and then into the panko mixture. Ensure both sides are evenly coated. Place on a plate or platter as you repeat the process with all the chicken.
In a 10-inch skillet heat 4 tbsp of oil over medium heat. To test the oil you can dip the back of a wooden spoon into the oil and if bubbles form immediately, the oil is ready.

Place a prepared chicken cutlet in the hot oil for 2–3 minutes on each side. If you notice the oil getting too hot and the chicken browning too quickly, simply turn the heat down to medium-low. Don't overcrowd the pan. I fry 2 cutlets at a time.
Transfer the cooked chicken to a paper towel lined plate to absorb any excess oil. And sprinkle with flaky salt. Repeat with all the chicken cutlets.
recipes.prepNotes: To test the oil, dip the back of a wooden spoon into the oil; if bubbles form immediately, the oil is ready. Don't overcrowd the pan — fry 2 cutlets at a time. Turn heat to medium-low if browning too quickly.

recipes.title: Keto Fudge Brownie
instructions: Blend all dry ingredients into a powder. Pour into bowl
Beat egg whites till stiff
Add yogurt, vanilla and almond milk to dry ingredients and mix.
Add egg whites, 1/3 at a time and whip up
add parchment paper to a 9x9 pan. pour batter into pan.
Add topping (chocolate chips, pb chips, etc)
Bake 190 deg for 18 minutes
recipes.prepNotes: Can substitute water for almond milk

recipes.title: Strawberry Sherbert
instructions: Blend with immersion blender.  Freeze
Spin on Sorbet setting

recipes.title: Sugar Free Whipped Cream
instructions: Chill your bowl and beaters in the freezer for ~10 min (helps it whip faster).
Pour in cold cream, sweetener, and vanilla.
Beat on medium-high until soft peaks form, then continue to stiff peaks (about 2–4 min). Don't overbeat or it'll turn to butter.
If using xanthan gum, sprinkle it in near the end while beating.
recipes.prepNotes: Keeps in the fridge 2–3 days; re-whip gently if it deflates.

recipes.title: Air Fryer Chicken Meatballs
instructions: Mix: In a bowl, combine 500 grams ground chicken, 1 large egg, 40 grams breadcrumbs, 2 garlic cloves, minced, ½ onion, finely grated, 1 teaspoons salt, ½ teaspoons black pepper, 1 teaspoons paprika, and 1 teaspoons dried oregano. Mix with your hands just until combined — don't overwork or they turn dense.
Shape: Roll into balls about 3–4 cm across (roughly 16–18 meatballs). Wet hands slightly to stop sticking.
Preheat: Preheat air fryer to 190°C.
Cook: Arrange meatballs in a single layer, not touching. Lightly spray with 1 tablespoons olive oil, for spraying. Air fry at 190°C for 11–11 minutes, shaking the basket once halfway.
Check: Confirm internal temp hits 74°C (or cut one open — no pink, juices run clear). Rest 2 minutes before serving.
recipes.prepNotes: Ground chicken is wetter than beef — if the mix feels too loose to roll, add breadcrumbs 1 tbsp at a time. No breadcrumbs? Swap in rolled oats or almond flour (almond flour keeps it lower-carb). They keep 4 days refrigerated and freeze well cooked. ~28 g protein per serving. Don't overmix — main failure mode with poultry meatballs; they get rubbery fast. Temp check matters more than time — chicken dries out past 74°C, so pull them right at target.

recipes.title: Simple Cooked Apples
instructions: Melt butter in a small pan over medium heat.
Add chopped apples, brown sugar, and water.
Cook 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until apples are soft and fork-tender.
Let cool completely before blending.
recipes.prepNotes:
- Chop small so they soften evenly and blend smoothly.
- Softer varieties like Fuji, Gala, or Golden Delicious break down nicely

recipes.title: Candied Maple Walnuts
instructions: Toast walnuts in a dry skillet over medium heat, 3-5 minutes, until fragrant.

Add maple syrup and salt.  Keep stirring over medium heat, 2-3 minutes. The syrup will bubble, thicken, then turn from glossy to a matte, sandy coating that clings to the nuts. That crystallized look is the signal it's done — pull it immediately.
Spread on parchment paper in a single layer. Separate any clumps.
Let cool completely—they'll harden and get crisp.
Chop once cool.
recipes.prepNotes:
- Watch closely at the end; maple burns fast.
- Add a pinch of cinnamon or cayenne for extra flavor.
- Make sure they're fully cooled and crisp before mixing into the ice cream, so they stay crunchy.

recipes.title: Hazelnut Chocolate Coffee Ice Cream
instructions: Combine all ingredients.
One spin on lite ice cream setting.
Top with TJs dark chocolate covered espresso beans.
recipes.prepNotes:
*cold brew substitute:  French Press
1. Coarsely grind coffee, use a ~1:8 ratio (e.g., 40g coffee to 320g cold water).
2. Stir grounds into cold water in the press.
3. Steep 12–18 hours at room temp or in the fridge.
4. Press the plunger and pour off. Done—smooth, low-acid concentrate.

* monk fruit w/allulose: straight allulose will be less sweet gram-for-gram—you'll likely need more. Start by roughly doubling the amount (~10g) and taste 

* hazelnut syrup substitute (not rescaled):
1/4 tsp Hazelnut extract (start here—it's potent; taste and add a drop more if needed)
Extra sweetener:  Add ~3–5g to replace the syrup's sweetness
Mix it in with everything else, then taste the base before spinning and adjust to your liking.

recipes.title: Raspberry Lemonade Sorbet
instructions: Add raspberries and lemonade to the tub, filling to the max line.
Freeze
Process using the sorbet option.
Run 1 respin for the best texture.

recipes.title: Pistachio Ice Cream
instructions: Blend Ingredients – Combine all ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.
Freeze – Pour into tub, cover, and freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin – use the “Ice Cream” setting.
Respin if needed – If crumbly, add 1-2 tablespoons of milk and respin.
Add Mix-Ins – Stir in chopped pistachios, and select the Mix-In function.
recipes.prepNotes:
* If too crumbly, add 1-2 tablespoons of milk. Respin until smooth.
* Taste your base before freezing. It should be slightly sweeter than your preference.

Substitutions:
Replace half-and-half – Use equal parts heavy cream and whole milk.
Swap cottage cheese – Use Greek yogurt for a tangy twist.
Skip condensed milk – Use honey or maple syrup for a natural sweetener.
Add-ins: Dark chocolate chips

Variations:
Dubai Chocolate Ice Cream – Add cocoa powder for a chocolatey twist. 
Pistachio Chocolate Ice Cream – Mix in chocolate chips or a fudge swirl. 
Honey Pistachio Ice Cream – Drizzle honey for a floral sweetness. 
Cardamom Pistachio Ice Cream – Add a pinch of cardamom for Middle Eastern vibes. 
Salted Caramel Pistachio Ice Cream – Swirl in homemade caramel sauce. 
Espresso Pistachio Ice Cream – Add a shot of espresso for a bold twist. 
Coconut Pistachio Ice Cream – Use coconut milk instead of half-and-half.

recipes.title: Old-Fashioned Chocolate Ice Cream

ingredients.name: Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste

instructions: Dissolve Cocoa Powder: In a small bowl, mix the cocoa powder with a few tablespoons of hot water until smooth. Let it cool slightly.
Blend: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until smooth.
Freeze: Pour into a container and freeze for 24 hours.
Spin: select the Ice Cream function.
Respin if needed: If crumbly, add 1-2 tablespoons of milk and respin.
recipes.prepNotes:
* If the ice cream turns out crumbly, add 1-2 tablespoons of milk and respin.
* For a rich chocolate flavor, use high-quality cocoa powder and dissolve it in hot water first. Blending cocoa powder directly with other ingredients may take longer and create excess bubbles in the mixture.

Substitutions:
Replace Half and Half ➝ Use an equal mix of heavy cream and whole milk. However, be cautious when using heavy cream —over-spinning may cause it to turn into butter.
Replace Cottage Cheese ➝ Use Greek yogurt for a tangy twist.

Fun Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
Classic: crushed Oreos, peanut butter swirl, chocolate chunks, fresh strawberries (chocolate-covered strawberry vibes).
Extra treat: brownie chunks, chopped Reese’s, hot fudge, salted caramel.
Crunchy: chopped nuts, graham crackers, toffee bits, or crushed candy bars like Snickers, Heath, or Butterfinger.

recipes.title: Chocolate Brownie Ice Cream

ingredients.name: vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste

instructions: Whisk cocoa powder with hot water until smooth.
Blend cocoa mixture with all remaining ingredients (except brownies) until fully smooth.
Pour into a tub and freeze 24 hours.
Spin on Ice Cream.
Respin once until creamy.
Add brownie chunks and run Mix-In cycle.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Respin is expected — this isn’t a failure, it’s the process
* Add mix-ins only using the Mix-In cycle

recipes.title: Pumpkin Spice Latte Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup half and half (equal parts milk and heavy cream)

instructions: Blend the ingredients on high until completely smooth and creamy — no lumps or grainy bits.
Pour the mixture into a tub, stopping just below the max fill line. Freeze flat for at least 24 hours.
Spin Select the Ice Cream function and let it run through the full cycle.
Check texture: If it looks crumbly after the first spin, press Respin until smooth and creamy. You can add a splash of half & half before respinning for extra creaminess.
Add mix-ins (optional): Once you’ve hit your ideal texture, add any mix-ins — like crushed gingersnaps, caramel bits, or toasted pecans — and run the Mix-In cycle.
Scoop it right away or refreeze for 30 minutes if you prefer it firmer.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
Use heavy cream + milk instead of half & half for extra richness
Swap cottage cheese for Greek yogurt (full fat) or soft cream cheese
Replace brown sugar with maple syrup, monk fruit, or stevia
Add mix-ins like crushed gingersnaps, toasted pecans, caramel swirl

Flavor Variations to Try
Pumpkin Spice Latte Ice Cream — up the espresso a bit for a bolder coffee hit
Maple Pumpkin — swap or drizzle in maple syrup
Caramel Pumpkin Spice — swirl caramel sauce toward the end
Pumpkin Chocolate Chip — fold in mini chocolate chips
Pumpkin Pecan Crunch — add toasted pecans or candied nuts

recipes.title: Pumpkin Cheesecake Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ½  cup equivalent sweetened condensed milk

instructions: Blend all but the graham crackers until ultra smooth.
Pour into tub, freeze 24 hours
Remove, spin on Ice Cream mode.
Create a small well, add 1 Tbsp extra liquid if needed, respin.
Add graham cracker crumbs to the well, hit mix-in mode or stir gently.
Serve immediately or level and freeze for future respinning.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Use full-fat half and half and cream cheese for better texture
* Let the pint rest 2–3 minutes before spinning if extremely firm
* Graham cracker substitution: Lotus Biscoff, Petit Beurre, Digestive biscuits

Substitution and Additions
* Swap cream cheese for cottage cheese for a lighter tang
* Use evaporated milk or a half heavy cream / half milk combo
* Add a splash of maple syrup for extra fall flavor
* Mix in pecans, toffee bits, or white chocolate chips
* Boost spice with extra cinnamon or a pinch of ginger

recipes.title: Maple Walnut Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ½ cup chopped candied maple walnuts (see recipe)

instructions: Blend half and half, condensed milk, cream cheese, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt until smooth.
Pour into a tub and freeze 24 hours.
Spin using Ice Cream setting.
If crumbly, add 1 Tbsp half-and-half and Respin.
Add candied walnuts using Mix-In cycle. (see recipe for walnuts)
recipes.prepNotes:
* Half-and-half prevents the “butter problem” heavy cream can cause.
* Always blend cream cheese fully for a silky base.
* Add nuts after spinning to keep them crisp.

No candied nuts? Toasted walnuts work great too.
Go full fall mode: Add ¼ teaspoon cinnamon or nutmeg to the base before freezing.

recipes.title: Apple Cinnamon Pie Ice Cream
instructions: Add cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, apples, half and half, brown sugar, vanilla, cinnamon, and sea salt to a blender.
Blend until completely smooth. This matters.
Pour the mixture into a container
Freeze on a flat surface for at least 24 hours.
Spin on Ice Cream. If crumbly, respin.
Add  graham crackers using the Mix-In cycle.
recipes.prepNotes:
Add a pinch of nutmeg if you want more spice depth

Canned apples substitute - home-cooked apples (see recipe). - Since you're adding sugar here, you may want to slightly reduce the brown sugar in the ice cream base
Cool apples fully—warm apples will affect your base temperature before freezing.

Toppings:
Caramel drizzle
Cinnamon sugar sprinkle
Chopped toasted pecans
A dollop of whipped cream if you’re feeling fancy

recipes.title: Chai Tea Latte Ice Cream
instructions: Steep the tea: Add chai tea bags to 1/2 cup hot water. Steep 5-7 minutes until dark and concentrated. Remove tea bags and let cool to room temperature.
Blend the base: Add cooled tea, half and half, cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, salt, and cinnamon to a blender. Blend on high for 30 seconds until completely smooth.
Freeze: Pour into container. Freeze on a flat surface for 24 hours.
Spin: select **Ice Cream**.
Check texture: If crumbly or powdery (normal for tea-based ice cream), make a small well in the center and add 1-2 tablespoons half and half or milk. Select **Respin**.
Serve: Scoop immediately for soft-serve texture, or freeze 20-30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitutions:
* Half and half: Equal parts heavy cream and whole milk if you don't have it
* Cottage cheese: Greek yogurt (thicker texture) or cream cheese (richer but harder to blend)
* Sugar-free: Use sugar-free sweetened condensed milk
* Extra spicy: Add ¼ teaspoon ground cardamom or ginger
* Dirty chai: Add 1 teaspoon instant espresso powder to the base before freezing

Mix-ins
* Candied pecans - Adds crunch and a caramel note
* Cinnamon graham cracker pieces - Like having chai with a cookie
* Chocolate chunks - Because chai + chocolate is a classic pairing

Toppings
* Whipped cream - Classic chai latte topping
* Cinnamon stick - Makes it look fancy
* Caramel drizzle - Adds sweetness and visual appeal
* Extra cinnamon dusting - Reinforces the spice
* Chopped pistachios - Adds color and a subtle nuttiness

Flavor Variations You Should Try
* Dirty Chai. Add 1 teaspoon instant espresso powder or 2 tablespoon cooled strong espresso to the base before freezing. The coffee + chai combo is unreal. If you love coffee ice cream, you'll love this.
* Vanilla Chai. Double the vanilla extract (use 1 teaspoon total). This makes it taste more like a vanilla chai latte - mellower on the spice, heavier on the sweet vanilla notes.
* Extra Spiced Chai. Add ¼ teaspoon ground cardamom and ¼ teaspoon ground ginger to the base. Or steep the tea for the full 7 minutes and add a pinch of black pepper for heat. Great if you like your chai seriously spicy.
* Maple Chai. Replace the sweetened condensed milk with ⅓ cup pure maple syrup + ¼ cup heavy cream. Gives it a fall flavor twist similar to maple walnut but with chai spices.

recipes.title: Plain Tart Frozen Yogurt
instructions: Blend – In a blender, mix Greek yogurt, honey, and lemon juice. Blend until smooth.
Freeze – Pour into a container and freeze for at least 24 hours.
Spin – select Lite Ice Cream or Yogurt
Check texture – If crumbly, add 1-2 tbsp milk and re-spin.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution and Additions
* Swap Greek yogurt → Use plain yogurt or full-fat yogurt for creaminess.
* Replace honey → Try maple syrup or monk fruit sweetener.
* Extra flavor → Add vanilla extract or peanut butter.

What Goes Well With Plain Tart Frozen Yogurt
* Build a Froyo Bowl: Start with 2 scoops, add fresh berries, a sprinkle of granola, and a drizzle of honey. Pinkberry at home.
* Make an Affogato: Pour a shot of hot espresso over a scoop of plain tart froyo. The hot-cold contrast is incredible.
* Top Warm Desserts: Serve alongside warm apple crisp, berry cobbler, or brownies. The tanginess cuts through the sweetness perfectly.
* Breakfast Frozen Yogurt: Top with nuts, seeds, and fresh fruit for a protein-packed breakfast treat.
* Sundae Style: Layer with fresh fruit, chocolate sauce, and whipped cream for a healthier sundae.

recipes.title: Classic Coffee Ice Cream
instructions: Blend half and half, espresso, cottage cheese, condensed milk, and vanilla until smooth
Freeze flat in a container for 24 hours
Spin using “Lite Ice Cream”  function
Respin if crumbly—add 1-2 tbsp milk if needed
Serve & Enjoy—grab a spoon and dig in!
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitutions
* Half & half - Use 50/50 whole milk + heavy cream
* Instant espresso - Brewed espresso concentrate or cold brew concentrate works too
* Cottage cheese - Try cream cheese or Greek yogurt (texture will vary)
* Sugar - Use unsweetened condensed coconut milk or a sugar-free version

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Mini chocolate chips – Classic combo with coffee, adds texture and sweetness
* Crushed Oreos or Biscoff – Like a dirty coffee float—so good
* Toffee bits or caramel chips – Sweetens and complements the coffee richness
* Chopped almonds or walnuts – Nutty crunch with a grown-up vibe
* Sea salt + chocolate drizzle – Sweet and salty fans, this one's for you

recipes.title: Maple Cinnamon Toast Crunch Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ⅔ cup Cinnamon Toast Crunch cereal

instructions: Add half and half, condensed milk, cottage cheese, maple syrup, vanilla, cinnamon powder, and salt to a blender. Blend until totally smooth.
Pour into a container up to the max fill line.
Freeze for 24 hours.
Spin on Ice Cream cycle.
If crumbly, Respin. Add 1–2 Tbsp half and half if needed.
Create a center tunnel, add Cinnamon Toast Crunch, and Mix-In.
Serve immediately or freeze briefly for a firmer scoop.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitutions
* Swap for Cinnamon Squares, or even granola.
* Add a swirl of extra maple syrup after the Mix-In for a ribbons effect.
* Fold in crushed pecans or walnuts for a maple-nutty vibe.
* Sub half the half-and-half with milk for a lighter base.

Topping & Mix-In Ideas
* Extra crushed cinnamon cereal after the Mix-In cycle
* A drizzle of warm maple syrup on top
* Toasted walnuts or pecans
* A sprinkle of cinnamon sugar
* Whipped cream (because why not?)

recipes.title: Shamrock Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ½ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor
Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste

instructions: Blend Ingredients – Combine everything in a blender until smooth.
Freeze for 24 Hours – Pour into a container and freeze on a level surface.
Spin on Ice Cream Mode
Respin if Needed – If it’s crumbly, respin for the perfect texture.
Serve and Enjoy – Scoop it up and enjoy a creamy, minty treat!

recipes.title: Mint Cookies and Cream Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¼ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor (add more if you want)
Crushed oreo cookies add more if you want

instructions: Blend: Add all the ingredients to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a container. Freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin: Ice Cream mode
Respin (if needed): If the texture is crumbly, add 1–2 tablespoons of milk
Add mix-ins: mix in crushed Oreos
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitutions:
* Half & half - Mix equal parts heavy cream + whole milk
* Cottage cheese - Try Greek yogurt

Fun Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Crushed Oreos for extra crunch
* Mini chocolate chips for classic texture
* Brownie bits if you want it extra rich

recipes.title: Mint Chocolate Chip Ice Cream - 2

ingredients.name: ½ teaspoon Mint chocolate chip flavor
Vanilla extract or vanilla bean paste
Chocolate chips or mint chocolate chips (chopped)

instructions: In a blender, combine half and half, sweetened condensed milk, cottage cheese, vanilla, and mint flavoring. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a container. Freeze for at least 24 hours.
Spin on the Ice Cream setting
If too crumbly, add a splash of milk and respin.
Stir in chocolate chips or use Mix in function and enjoy!
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
* Half and Half – Use equal parts heavy cream and whole milk.
* Sweetened Condensed Milk – Try coconut condensed milk for a dairy-free version.
* Cottage Cheese – Use Greek yogurt or cream cheese for a tangy twist.

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Crushed Oreos (mint cookies & cream vibes)
* Extra chocolate chunks or shaved chocolate
* Chopped walnuts or almonds for crunch
* Shredded coconut (surprisingly good with mint)

recipes.title: Hot Honey Ice Cream

ingredients.name: Vanilla Extract or vanilla bean paste

instructions: Blend the ingredients: In a blender, combine half-and-half, hot honey, cottage cheese, vanilla, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a container. Freeze for 12-24 hours.
Spin on the “Ice Cream” setting.
Re-spin if needed: If the texture is crumbly, add 1-2 tbsp of milk and re-spin until creamy.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
* Cottage Cheese for Greek Yogurt – Similar protein boost, different tangy flavor.
* Heavy Cream Instead of Half-and-Half – For an extra rich texture.
* Add a Dash of Cinnamon – Enhances the warmth of the hot honey.

Flavor Variations to Try
* Honey Lavender – Add dried lavender for a floral touch.
* Chili Chocolate Honey – Mix in cocoa powder and extra chili.
* Hot Honey & Cinnamon Swirl – Swirl in cinnamon sugar.
* Spicy Peanut Butter Honey – Add peanut butter for a nutty kick.
* Maple Hot Honey – Replace honey with maple syrup.
* Lemon Honey Spice – Add a touch of lemon zest.
* Chai Honey – Infuse with chai spices like cardamom and cloves.
* Hot Honey Espresso – A coffee-infused twist!

What Goes Well with Hot Honey Ice Cream?
* Warm Apple Pie – A spicy-sweet contrast.
* Grilled Peaches – Smoky and caramelized goodness.
* Buttermilk Waffles – Perfect sweet-savory breakfast treat.
* Dark Chocolate Brownies – Deep, rich flavors complement the spice.
* Chili-Spiced Nuts – Extra crunch and heat.
* Vanilla Bean Shortbread Cookies – Buttery balance to the spice.

recipes.title: Cookies and Cream Ice Cream
instructions: Add the milk, heavy cream, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, and salt directly to the container. Use a small whisk, milk frother, or spoon to mix until fully combined and smooth.
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours until completely solid.
Process using the Ice Cream setting.
If the texture looks crumbly or powdery, add 1 tablespoon milk and run Respin once.
Create a hole in the center of the ice cream, add the broken Oreo cookies, and run the Mix-In cycle.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
* Different Oreo flavors - Golden Oreos give you a vanilla-on-vanilla situation that's sweeter and more caramel-forward. Mint Oreos add a cool finish. Birthday Cake Oreos lean fun and festive. The base works with any of them - just keep the quantity the same.
* Dairy-free - Swap the milk for oat milk and the heavy cream for full-fat coconut cream. Use sweetened condensed coconut milk in place of regular. The texture will be slightly softer but still creamy.
* More chocolate - Add a tablespoon of chocolate sauce into the well before your Oreos go in. It swirls through the base during the mix-in and gives you a little extra chocolate hit.

recipes.title: Peanut Butter Cup Ice Cream
instructions: Blend the base. Add the milk, heavy cream, sweetened condensed milk, creamy peanut butter, and salt to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, about 45 to 60 seconds, making sure the peanut butter is fully incorporated with no streaks.
Pour and freeze. Pour the mixture into your container. Cover with the lid and freeze for 24 hours, or until completely solid.

Thaw slightly, then spin. Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit at room temperature for about 5 minutes. Process on the Ice Cream setting. If it looks crumbly after the first spin, run a Re-Spin.
Add the mix-ins. Make a hole in the center of the ice cream with a spoon. Chop the mini peanut butter cups and add them to the center. Process on the Mix-In setting.
recipes.prepNotes:
Use standard creamy peanut butter, not natural. Natural peanut butter separates and can make the base oily or uneven after freezing. Jif, Skippy, or any standard creamy peanut butter works best here.

Flavor Variations to Try
* Extra chocolate: Add a tablespoon of cocoa powder to the base before blending for a chocolate peanut butter cup situation.
* Peanut butter swirl: After the first spin, drop an extra tablespoon of peanut butter into the well before adding your mix-ins. Run the mix-in setting and you'll get a peanut butter ribbon through the whole pint.

Different mix-ins: Reese's Pieces, chocolate chips, or crushed chocolate cookies, Peanut butter cups plus mini chocolate chips together is especially good.

recipes.title: Black Sesame Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk the whole milk, heavy cream, sugar, black sesame paste, vanilla extract, and salt in a bowl or large measuring cup until the sugar dissolves and the sesame paste is fully blended. The mixture should look smooth and evenly dark gray.
Pour the mixture into a container, making sure it stays below the max fill line.
Freeze on a flat, level surface for at least 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit on the counter for 5 minutes before processing.
Run the Ice Cream cycle.
Check the texture after the first spin. If it looks crumbly, powdery, or uneven, run the Re-spin cycle. Add 1 tablespoon of milk before respinning if needed.
Serve right away for a softer texture, or freeze for 30 minutes if you want firmer, more classic ice cream scoops.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Whisk until the paste is fully dissolved. Black sesame paste can clump if the milk is cold. If you're having trouble getting it smooth, warm the milk slightly (not hot, just room temp or a little above) before mixing. The paste disperses much more easily.

* Taste your paste first. Black sesame pastes vary a lot by brand. Some are sweetened, some are unsweetened, some are intensely bitter. Taste it before you build the base and adjust the sugar accordingly. This one step saves you from a batch that's too bitter or too sweet.

**Substitute in a pinch:** Tahini works, but you'll lose the signature dark color and get a milder flavor.

Variations
* Toasted sesame swirl - Toast a tablespoon of whole black sesame seeds in a dry pan for 2 minutes until fragrant, then stir them into the spun ice cream using the Mix-In function. Adds crunch and extra flavor.
* Black sesame and white chocolate - After spinning, use Mix-In to fold in white chocolate chips or chopped white chocolate. The sweetness of white chocolate plays off the slight bitterness of the sesame really well.
* Black sesame and miso - Add half a teaspoon of white miso to the base before freezing. It deepens the savory quality of the ice cream and makes it more complex without tasting like miso soup.
* Coconut black sesame - Swap the heavy cream for full-fat coconut cream. Gives it a slightly tropical edge and pairs naturally with the nuttiness of the sesame.

recipes.title: Blackberry Cheesecake Ice Cream
instructions: Blend it up: Blend all ingredients in a blender or food processor until smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a container and freeze on a level surface for at least 24 hours.
Spin: Use the “Lite Ice Cream” or “Gelato” mode to spin.
Re-spin: If crumbly, add 1–2 tbsp milk and re-spin until creamy.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
* Replace half and half with equal parts heavy cream + whole milk.
* Add Lemon zest for a citrusy pop
* Add Graham cracker crumbs mixed in after spinning for true cheesecake vibes

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Fresh Blackberries: Pile them on for extra berry goodness.
* Lemon Zest: Brightens the creamy base.
* Graham Cracker Crumbs: Total cheesecake-in-a-cone moment.
* Mini Chocolate Chips: Adds a little indulgence.
* Honey Drizzle: Enhances the fruit’s natural sweetness.

recipes.title: Watermelon Ice Cream (2 ingredients)
instructions: Blend watermelon and sweetened condensed milk until smooth.
Pour into a container.
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Spin using “Lite Ice Cream” mode.
Re-spin if crumbly.
Scoop and enjoy your creamy watermelon treat!
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitutions
* Condensed milk - Use full-fat coconut milk for a dairy-free version. Add sugar.
* Add a pinch of sea salt to balance the sweetness

recipes.title: Sugar Free Blackberry Sorbet

ingredients.name: Fresh blackberries (You can use frozen ones too!)
monk fruit (adjust according to your taste)

instructions: Blend the Ingredients – In a blender or food processor, puree blackberries, monk fruit, water, and lemon juice until smooth.
Strain If Needed – If you want a seed-free sorbet, strain the mixture.
Pour into a container and freeze for 24 hours.
Spin to Perfection – select the sorbet setting, and spin.
Respin If Needed – If it’s too crumbly, add a splash of water and spin again.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitution
Lemon Juice - Use lime juice or orange juice instead. You can also skip it if you want.
Want It Creamier? Add a splash of coconut milk.
No monk fruit? Swap with honey or maple syrup or regular granulated sugar if you prefer.

recipes.title: Creamy Strawberry Sorbet
instructions: Blend the Ingredients: Start by blending strawberries, sugar, and water in a blender or food processor until smooth.

Heat the Mixture: Gently heat the blended mixture in a saucepan. Warm it without boiling. Stir in a slurry made from tapioca starch or cornstarch mixed with water, stirring continuously until slightly thickened.
Pour your mixture into a container. Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours for best results.
Spin using the sorbet setting.
Respin if Needed: If it's not creamy enough, simply respin once more.
recipes.prepNotes:
Macerate strawberries by tossing them with sugar and allowing them to sit for about 30 minutes before blending. This enhances flavor and sweetness.

Substitutions
Sweetener Alternatives: Substitute sugar with monk fruit, maple syrup or honey if preferred.

recipes.title: Blueberry Cheesecake Ice Cream
instructions: Macerate – Sprinkle sugar over the blueberries and let them sit for about 10 minutes to release their natural juices and enhance the flavor.
Blend – Add the macerated blueberries and the remaining ingredients to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Freeze – Pour into container, cover, and freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin – use the Ice Cream mode to churn.
Respin – If the texture is crumbly, respin until it’s smooth and creamy.
recipes.prepNotes:
The Macerating Trick (Don’t Skip This!)
Macerating is tossing fruit with a little sugar and let it sit. The sugar pulls out the juices, which intensifies the flavor and creates a natural syrup.

For this recipe, macerating means deeper purple color, stronger blueberry flavor, smoother blending, and more even sweetness throughout the ice cream.

To do it, add the blueberries to a bowl, sprinkle them with the 2 tablespoons of sugar from the recipe, and gently toss. Let them sit for about 10 minutes, or up to 30 minutes if you want even more juice. Use both the berries and all that syrup in your ice cream base.

Substitution and Additions
* Replace half and half → Use equal parts heavy cream and whole milk.
* Replace condensed milk → Use honey or maple syrup for a natural sweetener.
* Replace fresh blueberries → Use frozen blueberries, thawed, for a similar effect.
* A squeeze of lemon juice for extra tanginess.
* A sprinkle of graham cracker crumbs for more cheesecake vibes.
* A swirl of blueberry sauce for extra flavor.

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Graham Cracker Crumbles 🍪 – Adds that classic cheesecake crust texture.
* Fresh Blueberries 🫐 – Extra juicy bursts of fruit in every bite.
* Lemon Zest 🍋 – Enhances the tartness of the cheesecake base.

recipes.title: Cherry Ice Cream
instructions: Blend: Add all ingredients to a blender or food processor. Blend until completely smooth.
Freeze: Pour the mixture into a container. Level the top, cover, and freeze for 24 hours on a flat surface.
Spin on the Ice Cream function.
Respin (If Needed): If the texture is crumbly, add 1–2 tablespoons of milk and use the Respin function.
recipes.prepNotes:
Blend the cottage cheese until COMPLETELY smooth—any lumps will show up in your final texture
Use room temperature cream cheese if swapping it for cottage cheese
Don't skip the vanilla—it rounds out the cherry flavor beautifully

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Dark chocolate chunks (Cherry Garcia style!),  mini chocolate chips, chopped almonds, crushed vanilla wafers, graham cracker pieces, crushed Oreos, granola clusters, hot fudge sauce, cream cheese frosting swirl, fresh strawberry pieces, blueberries, toasted coconut flakes

Flavor Variations You Can Try Next
* Cherry Chocolate Chip – Mix in chocolate chips after spinning
* Cherry Cheesecake – Add cream cheese and crushed graham crackers
* Black Cherry Vanilla – Use dark cherries and real vanilla bean
* Coconut Cherry – Use coconut milk and toasted coconut
* Protein Cherry Ice Cream – Add a scoop of plain protein powder

======================
***Fresh-cherry version****

- 1 cup fresh cherries, pitted
- 1½ cup half and half
- 8–9 tablespoons sugar (up from 6)
- ½ cup cottage cheese
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- ¼ teaspoon almond extract (optional, for that classic cherry flavor)
- Pinch of salt (enhances flavor)

**Method:**
1. **Macerate:** Toss pitted cherries with 2 tablespoons of the sugar. Let sit 15–20 min to draw out juice and soften.
2. **Blend:** Add everything (cherries + their juice) to the blender. Blend until smooth.
3. **Taste:** Adjust sugar—the base should taste slightly too sweet before freezing (cold dulls sweetness).
4. **Freeze:** Pour into container, level, cover, freeze 24 hours on a flat surface.
5. **Spin** on the Ice Cream function.
6. **Respin** if crumbly—add 1–2 tablespoons milk.

**Optional for less iciness:** Instead of macerating, simmer the cherries with the 2 tablespoons sugar for 5 min into a quick compote, then cool completely before blending. This reduces water content and deepens the cherry flavor.

recipes.title: Mixed Berry Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup mixed berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries)

instructions: Add the half and half, cottage cheese, sweetened condensed milk, mixed berries, vanilla, and salt to a blender. Blend until completely smooth.
Pour the mixture into a container
Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Spin Select Ice Cream.
If the texture looks dry or crumbly after spinning, add a splash of half and half or milk and run Respin.
Serve right away for a softer texture, or freeze for 20 to 30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes:
Fresh berries have the brightest flavor, but they cost more and need prep. Frozen berries are cheaper, easy to keep on hand, and great year-round. The flavor may be slightly less bright, but once blended and spun, the difference is pretty small.

My pick is fresh when berries are in season and frozen the rest of the year. If using frozen, thaw them first before blending.

Substitutions:
* Mixed berries: Any combination of strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, blackberries
* Extra berry flavor: Add 1-2 tablespoons berry jam or preserves
* Dairy-free: Use full-fat coconut milk + coconut cream (skip cottage cheese)
* Sweeter: Add 2 tablespoons honey or sugar if your berries are tart
* Less sweet: Reduce sweetened condensed milk to ⅓ cup

recipes.title: Banana Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup whole milk or 2% milk

instructions: Add the banana to your container and mash with a fork until mostly smooth.
Pour in the sweetened condensed milk, milk, heavy cream, and vanilla extract. Stir until fully combined.
Freeze the pint on a flat surface for 24 hours.
Remove from freezer and let sit 1 to 2 minutes. Run warm water over the outside of the pint for 30 seconds.
Run **Ice Cream** cycle.
If crumbly, add 1 tablespoon milk and run **Respin**.
Serve immediately for soft-serve texture or freeze 15 to 20 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Wait for a spotty banana. A yellow banana with no spots will give you a bland, starchy result. Brown spots mean the starches have converted to sugar - that's what gives you deep banana flavor and a naturally sweeter base.
* Mash the banana well. Take an extra minute with the fork to get it as smooth as possible. Chunks of frozen banana don't fully process in the machine and can leave fibrous bits in the final texture.

Substitutions:
* Dairy-free - Use full-fat coconut milk in place of the milk and cream, and a dairy-free sweetened condensed milk. The texture will be slightly different but still creamy.
* Extra banana flavor - Add a drop of banana extract to the base alongside the vanilla. A little goes a long way.
* Lower sugar - Reduce the condensed milk to 3 tablespoons and add 2 tablespoons of allulose to compensate. Your base may freeze a touch harder.
* Peanut butter banana - Stir 2 tablespoons of peanut butter into the base before freezing. 
* Banana foster - Add a pinch of cinnamon and a pinch of brown sugar to the base, then mix in caramel sauce after spinning.
* Chocolate banana - Add 1 tablespoon of cocoa powder to the base for a chocolate-banana twist, or use the mix-in cycle to add mini chocolate chips.

Mix-In and Topping Ideas
* Mini chocolate chips (mix-in cycle or sprinkled on top)
* Crushed graham crackers for a banana cream pie vibe
* Sliced fresh banana and a drizzle of caramel sauce
* Chopped walnuts or pecans
* A swirl of peanut butter pressed into the top
* Hot fudge drizzled over the scoop

recipes.title: Strawberry Banana Ice Cream
instructions: Add the banana, strawberries, heavy cream, milk, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla extract, and salt to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, about 30 to 45 seconds.
Strain the base through a fine mesh strainer if you want a smoother, seed-free texture.
Pour the mixture into a container. Freeze on a level surface for at least 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit on the counter for 2 to 3 minutes.
Spin using the Ice Cream function
Respin once if the texture looks crumbly after the first spin.
recipes.prepNotes:
Banana - Use ripe or overripe banana for the sweetest, strongest flavor. Frozen and thawed banana works too.
Strawberries - Fresh or frozen both work. If using frozen, thaw and drain them first so the base doesn’t get watery. Hull the strawberries before blending and taste one first. If they’re not very sweet, add a little sugar or honey before freezing since the flavor mellows once frozen.

recipes.title: Strawberry Protein Frozen Yogurt

ingredients.name: strawberries (hulled, fresh or frozen and thawed)
½ cup whole milk or 2% milk
¾ cup full-fat or 2% Greek yogurt

instructions: Add the cottage cheese, strawberries, Greek yogurt, allulose, vanilla extract, and milk to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, with no visible cottage cheese curds or strawberry pieces.
Pour the mixture into a container. freeze on a level surface for 24 hours.
Remove the pint from the freezer and let it sit at room temperature for 2 to 3 minutes.
Process on the Lite Ice Cream setting for a lighter froyo texture, or use Ice Cream for a denser result.
Respin once if the texture is crumbly after the first spin.
Scoop and serve immediately, or refreeze for 20 to 30 minutes for firmer scoops.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Strawberries - fresh or frozen and thawed; frozen strawberries are often sweeter and more concentrated in flavor, which works really well here. Hull and drain them before blending.
* Blend the cottage cheese first, alone. It takes 20 extra seconds and guarantees a completely smooth base. Skipping this step is the most common reason cottage cheese bases come out slightly grainy.

Mix-Ins and Topping Ideas
* Fresh strawberry chunks - fold in after spinning for bursts of fresh fruit throughout the pint
* Granola - adds crunch and a little extra staying power;
* Honey drizzle - a light drizzle on top when serving
* Strawberry jam swirl - a teaspoon folded into the mix-in well gives you a jammy ribbon and intensifies the strawberry flavor

recipes.title: High Protein Strawberry Cheesecake Ice Cream

ingredients.name: Graham crackers (crushed or broken into small pieces)

instructions: Add the strawberries, cottage cheese, cream cheese, milk, allulose, and vanilla extract to a blender.
Blend until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy.
Pour the mixture into a container. Freeze on a level surface for 24 hours, or until completely solid.
Spin on the  Ice Cream setting.
Respin if the texture looks crumbly or powdery. Add a small splash of milk before respinning if needed.
Add crushed graham crackers to the center of the pint. Use the Mix-In setting to evenly incorporate the graham crackers.

recipes.title: Olive Oil Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ½ cup sweetened condensed milk (or adjust with condensed milk)

instructions: Blend: Combine half and half, olive oil, sweetened condensed milk, vanilla, and lemon in a blender. Blend until smooth and fully emulsified — meaning the olive oil is completely mixed in with no oily layer on top. It should look creamy and uniform, not separated.
Freeze for 24 hours on a level surface.
Spin: Run on “Ice Cream” mode
Respin: If the texture looks crumbly, make a small well in the center, add about 1 tablespoon of half and half, then respin until smooth.
recipes.prepNotes:
Topping & Mix-In Ideas
* Flaked sea salt or Maldon
* Crushed pistachios or almonds
* Dark chocolate shavings or nibs
* Citrus zest flakes
* Candied nuts or brittle

recipes.title: Cinnamon Churro Ice Cream
instructions: Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until completely smooth.
Pour into a 24 oz Ninja Creami Deluxe pint. Freeze 24 hours.
Spin using the Ice Cream setting.
If crumbly, press Respin. Add 1–2 tbsp half &half only if needed.
For add-ins, create a well in the center and run Mix-In.
Scoop and enjoy, or freeze 15 minutes to firm up.
recipes.prepNotes:
* Use softened cream cheese — cold blocks won’t blend smoothly.
* For stronger churro flavor, add a pinch more cinnamon.
* If your pint freezes rock hard, give it 5 minutes on the counter before spinning.

recipes.title: Snickerdoodle Ice Cream

ingredients.name: Optional: ¼ teaspoon of Cream of Tartar (adds tang)

instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Add mix-ins: Optional Snickerdoodle Cookie (Keto or store-bought), usually 1 serving per pint.
Top with whipped cream and cinnamon.

recipes.title: Lemon Bar Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¼ teaspoon of Cake Batter Extract

instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Optional mix-ins: For a more cake-like texture, use Bettergoods Lemon Chiffon Soft Baked Cookies or Lemon Bars (Keto/Regular or Quest Lemon Cake protein bars).
Make Lemon Icing: Mix 1 tablespoon of SF powdered sugar with 1/4 teaspoon of water and a couple drops of lemon juice/extract until an icing-like texture.
Drizzle icing over the top or mix with the whipped cream first.

recipes.title: Caramel Frapachino Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup of Fairlife Skim Milk
¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
brewed shot of expresso/coffee (2 oz) OR 1 scoop of Nespresso (if using this option keep the Fairlife Milk at 1 Cup)

instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Spin up on lite ice cream setting for extra fluffy smoothness.
Top with Non Fat Cool Whip and SF Caramel Syrup.
recipes.prepNotes: Vanilla base with an expresso shot spun up on lite ice cream setting for extra fluffy smoothness and topped with cool whip and caramel syrup. No mix-ins.

recipes.title: Chocolate Fudge Almond Toffee Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
PB2 Chocolate Chip Brownie mix OR Brownie Mix
of SF Skinny Girl Butter Toffee Syrup

instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Toast almonds in toaster oven (breakup first).
Add mix-ins: 14 grams toasted almonds, optional crumbled brownie bits.
Top with whipped cream, toffee bits, SF chocolate syrup, and brownie crumble.

recipes.title: Almond Fudge Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk
½ Tbsp of Dark Cocoa Powder (can use 1 Tbsp all dark if desired)

instructions: Mix all ingredients together.
Toast almonds in toaster oven (careful not to burn!).
Add mix-ins: 14 grams toasted almonds, 17 grams (1 inch square) Chocolate Fudge.
Optional: Squirt SF Chocolate Syrup in the bottom of the pint.
Optional toppings: Non Fat Cool Whip and SF Chocolate Syrup.

recipes.title: Toasted Coconut Almond Ice Cream

ingredients.name: ¾ cup unsweetened vanilla almond milk

instructions: Mix all ingredients together. Freeze.
Toast almonds in toaster oven, then crush (put in ziplock bag and crush with spoon).
Toast 2 Tbsp coconut flakes in toaster oven until slightly brown.
Add mix-ins: toasted crushed almonds,toasted coconut flakes, Chocolate Chips

recipes.title: Frozen Yogurt

ingredients.name: –50 g light agave syrup or pure maple syrup

instructions: Whisk all ingredients together (mixing in a separate bowl helps remove air lumps), then pour into the tub.
Freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Let sit on the bench 5–15 minutes before processing.
Process on the light ice cream function — generally needs two spins.
Store leftovers in the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Use a yogurt that's already sweetened. Approx. 48g protein with Danone high protein vanilla yogurt + high protein skim milk.

recipes.title: Cookie Dough Ice Cream

ingredients.name: For dough: 100 g light brown sugar
For dough: 20–40 ml + 1–2 tbsp milk of choice, as needed
For dough: 150–175 g heat-treated tapioca flour/starch
For dough: 1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
For dough: Pinch of fine salt
For dough: 100 g finely chopped dark chocolate

instructions: Vanilla ice cream: Whisk cream, condensed milk, milk, vanilla together in a bowl, let sit 5 minutes, whisk again.
Pour into a tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Cookie dough (30–60 min before processing): Brown the butter in a saucepan over low-medium heat until golden and nutty-smelling.
Pour over brown sugar in a heatproof bowl; cool 5 minutes.
Add 1 tbsp milk and stir to a syrupy brown liquid.
Add tapioca flour in batches until it forms a non-oily, rollable dough (add more milk if it crumbles).
Cool, then stir in chopped chocolate. Roll into balls or leave as crumbles; chill at least 10 minutes.
To assemble: Process the frozen ice cream base on the ice cream function (re-spin if balled up).
Fold chilled cookie dough into the spun ice cream in a large bowl.
Pack back into the tub; freeze ~20 minutes until firm. Serve.
recipes.prepNotes: Cookie dough should be prepared 30–60 minutes before processing the ice cream base.

recipes.title: Maple Pumpkin Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk ingredients together in a bowl; taste and adjust maple syrup to taste.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function; re-spin if needed.
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Homemade pumpkin puree is sweeter than canned, so needs less maple syrup. Stick to 75–100g maple syrup for proper texture.

recipes.title: Sticky Date Ice Cream

ingredients.name: –1 tsp vanilla bean extract, to taste
whey protein isolate with minimal sweetener

instructions: Place chopped dates in a heatproof bowl; sprinkle with baking soda and pour over boiling water. Stir and cool.
Transfer cooled date paste to a food processor with milk, salt, and vanilla; blend to desired consistency.
Return mixture to the bowl and whisk in the whey protein isolate (do not blend the protein — it gets too frothy).
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the light ice cream function; re-spin if needed. Serve immediately; return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: 3g baking soda for subtle flavour, 5g for a classic sticky date note. Do not substitute baking powder. Avoid high-sugar protein powders — too much sugar will prevent proper freezing.

recipes.title: Watermelon Sorbet
instructions: Blend all ingredients (and optional add-ins) until completely smooth.
Pour into the tub (don't exceed fill line) and freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the gelato function; re-spin once or twice as needed.
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on; re-spin on the gelato function before eating.

recipes.title: Coffee Ice Cream

ingredients.name: full fat milk (regular or lactose free)

instructions: Whisk all ingredients together in a bowl until completely smooth.
Pour into the tub; optionally chill in the fridge 3 hours to minimize the hump, then freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function; smooth a balled-up top with a spoon, or re-spin once if powdery (avoid over-processing — it can split the cream).

recipes.title: Coconut Ice Cream

ingredients.name: –200 g full fat or light coconut milk
Dash of vanilla bean paste or extract (optional)

instructions: Whisk all ingredients together; taste and adjust salt/vanilla.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 12–24 hours.
Let thaw 10 minutes before processing (optional, reduces need to re-spin).
Process on the ice cream function; re-process (no added liquid) if powdery or balled up.
Store leftovers in the freezer with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Choose coconut cream with ~20–25g fat per 100g.

recipes.title: Custard Ice Cream

ingredients.name: extra large eggs (150g out of shell)

instructions: Whisk eggs, sugar, and cream together in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat milk, vanilla, and salt in a saucepan until hot and just below a simmer.
Slowly pour the hot milk into the egg mixture while whisking.
Pour the combined mixture back into the saucepan and cook over low/medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens slightly (about 70°C, ~5 minutes).
If it starts to clump, remove from heat immediately and whisk vigorously; transfer out of the pot to stop cooking.
Cool slightly, then transfer to the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Spin on the ice cream function — needs two full spins to be perfect.
recipes.prepNotes: 4 eggs can be used for higher protein. Lower sugar to 50g if mixture is too soft.

recipes.title: Strawberry Ricotta Ice Cream
instructions: Blend strawberries to a smooth puree. Add remaining ingredients and pulse to combine until smooth and light pink.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function — needs 3 spins for ideal texture.

recipes.title: Chocolate Caramel Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk cocoa with boiling water in a saucepan to remove clumps.
Add remaining ingredients and whisk to combine; crush any caramel chunks.
Cook on low heat 2–5 minutes until caramel is dissolved. Cool slightly.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function — may need two spins.
Mix in chopped chocolate caramel eggs by hand (not via the mix-in function).
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Strawberry Ice Cream

ingredients.name: Optional: Few cracks freshly cracked black pepper

instructions: Hull, chop, and mash strawberries in a pot to release liquid.
Add allulose and stir to combine.
Cook over medium heat 2–3 minutes until allulose dissolves and mixture is a vibrant red coulis. Stir in any flavour boosters, then cool 15 minutes.
Transfer cooled mixture to the tub.
Add cream and mix thoroughly.
Freeze with the lid off, 16–24 hours.
Process on the ice cream function — should be perfect on first spin with 100–125g allulose.
Return leftovers to the fridge with the lid on.
recipes.prepNotes: Do not exceed 125g allulose — the mixture won't set. Less sweet strawberries may need more allulose; very sweet ones may need only 75–100g.

recipes.title: Sugar Free Lemon Sorbet
instructions: Mix allulose and stevia in a saucepan; rub in lemon zest.
Add water and salt, then lemon juice to taste.
Cook 1–3 minutes until allulose dissolves. Cool slightly.
Pour into the tub and freeze with the lid off, 24 hours.
Process on the sorbet function; re-spin once if needed (avoid spinning twice — can make it too soft).
Return leftovers to the freezer with the lid on; re-process before eating.
recipes.prepNotes: Use common sour lemons rather than Meyer/Lemonade lemons for the classic tart flavour.

recipes.title: Healthier Orange Creamsicle Protein Ice Cream
instructions: Combine ingredients and freeze 12–24 hours.
Spin on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Add 1 tbsp milk and re-spin 1–2x until smooth.
recipes.prepNotes: tastes like a copycat DQ Orange Julius

recipes.title: Apple Butter Biscoff Protein Ice Cream

ingredients.name: –2 tbsp apple butter or apple pie filling
Pinch of cinnamon/nutmeg or apple pie spice (not too much!)

instructions: Mix base ingredients together in a container and freeze 24 hours.
Once frozen, spin on the Lite Ice Cream setting.
Respin if needed.
Add in apple butter and Biscoff cookies as mix-ins and spin on respin.
recipes.prepNotes:
Substitute for salted caramel protein powder:
Vanilla protein powder
Pinch of salt
1/2 tap caramel extract OR 1 tsp caramel sauce
A little brown sugar for caramel-y depth

recipes.title: Sweet Potato Ice Cream
instructions: Whisk all ingredients together until smooth and spices are integrated.
Pour into the tub and freeze 16–24 hours with the lid off.
Process on the ice cream function. Re-spin for a fluffier texture if desired.
recipes.prepNotes: Sweet potato must be 100% puree, no added sugar/syrup. Canned sweet potato not tested.

recipes.title: Classic Ice Cream Base Recipe

ingredients.name: cream cheese (optional — makes the ice cream thicker)

instructions: Mix all ingredients together in a pint tub and freeze 24 hours
Once frozen, spin on the ice cream setting.
The ice cream will come out slightly crumbly — add 1 tbsp milk and respin.
recipes.prepNotes: For a thicker, still-creamy texture, freeze an additional 1–3 hours.

notes:
Ice cream:
* Use Full fat milk
!!!**** allulose. MORE than 1.5tsp
* xantan gum 1/8tsp + pinch
* Add a small amount of fat — even 5–10 g (a spoon of cream, egg yolk, or MCT/coconut)

* Shorten the room-temp rest to 3–5 min, or skip it if your base wasn't rock-hard. This is the biggest heat input you control.
